data.title: SOUN Stock Surges Early in 2026 – What Investors Should Watch Next
data.description:
SoundHound AI (SOUN) stock has started 2026 on a positive note, gaining over 13% so far this month. Investor optimism has been mainly fueled by the company's latest product unveiled at CES 2026. Meanwhile, the stock has also drawn increased Wall Street attention as analysts see meaningful upside in 2026. Looking ahead, investors are now focused on what could drive the next move. Key areas to watch include upcoming earnings, customer growth, partnerships, and broader trends in the AI and voice technology space.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

What Lies Ahead for SoundHound AI?

SoundHound AI is set to report its Q4 2025 results on February 26. The company continues to post losses as it invests in ambitious growth initiatives, but management has said it aims to reduce losses in the coming quarter and move closer to profitability.

The company expects full-year revenue to range between $165 million and $180 million, up from $84.7 million in 2024.

Wall Street's Views on SOUN for 2026

Recently, H.C. Wainwright's four-star-rated analyst Scott Buck reiterated his Buy rating on SOUN and maintained a $26 price target. He expects the company to deliver strong revenue growth through 2026. His $26 price target implies more than 100% upside for SOUN in 2026.

Buck noted that management expects to reach adjusted EBITDA break-even by late 2026, while continuing to prioritize growth. As a result, SoundHound may sacrifice short-term margins to drive revenue growth, supported by a strong balance sheet with $269 million in cash and no debt as of Q3 2025.

Earlier this month, Cantor Fitzgerald's four-star analyst Thomas Blakey reiterated his Buy rating on SOUN with a $15 price target. Blakey highlighted the company's strong execution, particularly its success in cross-selling and upselling voice and conversational AI solutions to existing customers. He also noted that expectations for SoundHound remain relatively low heading into 2026, leaving room for upside if the company continues to execute on its growth strategy.

What Is the Price Target for SOUN?

According to TipRanks, SOUN stock has received a Moderate Buy consensus rating, with five Buys and two Holds assigned in the last three months. SoundHound's average stock price target is $16.60, suggesting a potential upside of 48% from the current level.
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: Fed's Goolsbee Warns Inflation Will 'Come Roaring Back' if Fed Loses Independence
data.description:
Chicago Federal Reserve President Austan Goolsbee warned that undermining the Fed's independence could carry serious economic consequences amid a Justice Department probe into Fed Chair Jerome Powell over the central bank's $2.5 billion renovation of its Washington, D.C., headquarters.

"Anything that's infringing or attacking the independence of the central bank is a mess," Goolsbee told CNBC's "Squawk Box" on Thursday. "You're going to get inflation come roaring back if you try to take away the independence of the central bank."

*This is a developing story. Stay tuned for additional updates.*

data.title: Why Springview Stock Took Off Today and Where It May Be Headed
data.description:
Springview Holdings (NASDAQ:SPHL) caught traders' attention early today as the stock surged by triple digits — and the catalyst behind the move is a clear pivot toward sustainability. Shares spiked after the company announced a memorandum of understanding with Jiangsu GSO New Energy Technology, a China-based solar and green-energy solutions provider, to explore integrating rooftop solar and energy-efficient technologies into Springview's residential design-and-build projects in Singapore.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Under the proposed collaboration, Springview's Singapore subsidiary plans to pilot solar photovoltaic systems and related energy-efficiency solutions, leveraging GSO's technical expertise and engineering support. Springview, for its part, will continue to oversee project delivery, regulatory coordination, and homeowner engagement. While the agreement is preliminary and non-exclusive, it aligns neatly with Singapore's broader push toward renewable energy adoption and long-term carbon-reduction goals.

SPHL isn't covered by any Wall Street analysts. There are no bank ratings, price targets, or earnings models shaping expectations here. That leaves investors looking elsewhere for guidance – and one place to turn is TipRanks' technical report, which offers a data-driven look at how the stock is currently behaving beneath the surface.

According to TipRanks' technical snapshot, the 1-month outlook skews bearish overall, with the consensus landing on a Sell based on six bearish signals versus three bullish ones. Moving averages are especially negative, flashing a Strong Sell signal with four bearish indicators and no bullish readings. At the same time, momentum indicators tell a more nuanced story. The RSI (14) sits at 28.30, placing the stock firmly in oversold territory – a level that often attracts contrarian buyers. However, the Rate of Change (ROC) at –93.61 and Bull/Bear Power at –14.92 point to continued downside pressure.

Pivot data places the central pivot around $3.12, with classic support and resistance spanning roughly $0.98 to $4.32. Fibonacci levels cluster in similar zones, suggesting these price points could play an outsized role as traders gauge whether today's surge has legs – or fades back toward equilibrium.
data.author: Marty Shtrubel

data.title: S&P 500 Rebounds on Uplifting Jobless Claims Data
data.description:
Following two consecutive lower closes, the S&P 500 (SPX) is set to open Thursday's trading session higher on new labor market data.

For the week ended January 9, initial jobless claims fell by 9,000 to 198,000, below the consensus estimate of 215,000. Continuing jobless claims, which trail initial claims by a week, decreased by 19,000 to 1.884 million, also below expectations of 1.897 million.

data.title: ASML Stock Surged on an Analyst Upgrade & Price Target Increase
data.description:
ASML (ASML) stock soared on Thursday after analysts weighed in on the advanced semiconductor equipment systems company following a strong earnings report from one of its customers.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Let's start with a dive into the latest analyst updates for ASML stock.

  • KGI Securities analyst Leon Chen upgraded ASML stock to an Outperform rating from a Hold rating and set a $1,415 price target for the shares, suggesting a possible 11.97% upside.
  • Five-star Wells Fargo analyst Joseph Quatrochi reiterated a Buy rating for ASML stock and raised his price target to $1,450 from $1,140, implying a potential 14.74% upside for the shares.

Quatrochi's increased price target for ASML is tied to an update for the firm's bottom-up Semi industry model. He highlighted Industrial and Electrical leading AI indicators, AI token-driven supply/demand model, and WFE die-to-wafer models as reasons for the new price target.

To go along with all of this, semiconductor company TSMC (TSM) just reported incredibly strong earnings for Q4 2025. That's important to ASML as TSMC is one of its top customers.

ASML Stock Movement Today

ASML stock was up 6.57% in pre-market trading on Thursday, following a 0.51% drop yesterday. The shares have rallied 18.12% year-to-date and 68.43% over the past 12 months.

Despite the analysts' updates and earnings news, shares of ASML haven't seen much activity today. As of this writing, more than 240,000 shares have changed hands, compared to a three-month daily average trading volume of about 1.3 million units.
data.author: William White

data.title: TSM Stock Wins a New Street-High Price Target After Blockbuster Results
data.description:
Needham's top analyst Charles Shi raised his price target on Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM) after its blockbuster fourth quarter results and above-consensus guidance. Shi kept his Buy rating and lifted the price target from $360 to a new Street-high of $410, implying 25.3% upside potential from current levels.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

TSM shares are rallying in pre-market trading after the contract chip manufacturer reported a 35% jump in Q4 profit, surpassing estimates, driven by robust demand for AI chips. For Q4FY25, TSM posted NT$505.74 billion ($16 billion) in net income and diluted EPS of NT$19.50 ($3.14 per ADR). Meanwhile, revenue increased 25.5% year-over-year to NT$1.046 trillion ($33.73 billion), beating the expected NT$1.034 trillion.

Shi is a five-star analyst on TipRanks, ranking #313 out of 12,040 analysts tracked. He has a 58% success rate and an average return per rating of 27.50%.

Analyst Raises Estimates on Bullish Outlook

Shi highlighted the following key upgrades in TSMC's guidance.

  • 2026 Revenue Growth: Projected near 30%, surpassing the Street's 25% estimate and his own prior 25% model.
  • 2026 CapEx: Expected at $52-$56 billion, above his prior $50 billion forecast.
  • AI Revenue CAGR (through 2029): Raised to mid-to-high 50%, from previous mid-40%.
  • Long-Term Revenue CAGR: Now "approaching" 25%, up from 20%.
  • Through-Cycle Gross Margin: Elevated to "56% and higher," from "53% and higher."

Shi noted that TSMC's updated outlook significantly outpaces prior Street consensus and his own model. In turn, he revised forecasts upward, projecting TSMC's total 2027 revenue to approach $200 billion, up from the previous $182 billion, and AI revenue to hit $120 billion (previously $90 billion). This justifies the new $410 price target amid robust AI-driven momentum.

Is TSM a Good Stock to Buy?

Analysts remain highly optimistic about Taiwan Semiconductor's long-term prospects. On TipRanks, TSMC has a Strong Buy consensus rating based on seven Buys and one Hold rating. The average Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing price target of $320.61 implies nearly 2% downside potential from current levels. Over the past year, TSM shares have gained over 58%.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Stock Futures Trade Mixed, Chip Stocks Rise on TSMC Earnings
data.description:
U.S. stock futures traded mixed on Thursday morning after Big Tech names dragged down major indexes in the previous trading session. Chip stocks trended higher after Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing or TSMC (TSM) reported impressive results. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) and the S&P 500 (SPX) were up 0.92% and 0.41%, respectively, while those on the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were down 0.09%, at 8:42 a.m. EST on January 15.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

During Wednesday's regular trading, the three major indexes closed in the red, marking a second consecutive day of losses for them. The S&P 500 and the Dow Jones declined 0.53% and 0.09%, respectively, while the Nasdaq Composite fell 1.00%.

Yesterday, Chinese authorities told domestic firms to steer clear of around 12 U.S. and Israeli software vendors on national security grounds, leading to a slump in software stocks. Microsoft (MSFT), Meta (META), and Amazon (AMZN) each dropped more than 2%. Oracle (ORCL) and Broadcom each slid 4%. Moreover, NVDA (NVDA) shares dropped 1.4% after China instructed domestic firms to avoid H200 purchases. U.S. banks were also among the biggest laggards.

Turning to trending stocks, Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing shares jumped about 7% in pre-market trading after the chip company reported a 35% jump in the fourth quarter profit, thanks to AI-led demand for its chips. Shares of ASML Holding (ASML), a chip equipment manufacturer and a supplier for TSMC, were up 6%. Also, Nvidia, Advanced Micro Devices (AMD), and Broadcom (AVGO) stocks were up 1.5%, 3.05%, and about 3%, respectively.

Meanwhile, Goldman Sachs (GS) stock fell more than 1% after the investment bank reported its fourth-quarter results. In contrast, Dell Technologies (DELL) stock was up nearly 2% as Barclays upgraded the tech stock to Buy from Hold, citing strength in AI server orders.

Calix (CALX) – Calix provides cloud and software platforms that help service providers deliver broadband and smart home services. Today, J.P. Monday.com (MNDY) – This company offers a cloud-based work operating system that helps teams plan, track, and manage projects and workflows. Today, Jefferies analyst Brent Thill maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a price target of $260 per share. In the last three months, all eight Top Analysts covering the stock have rated it a Buy. Taken together, their 12-month price targets imply an upside of about 81.65%.
